1: #!/usr/bin/env node
2: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
3: // MODULE: Continuity Freshness Validator
4: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
5: 
6: import { execFileSync } from 'node:child_process';
7: import fs from 'node:fs';
8: import path from 'node:path';
9: import * as yaml from 'js-yaml';
10: import { isMainModule } from '../lib/esm-entry.js';
11: import {
12:   buildContinuityFingerprint,
13:   ZERO_CONTINUITY_FINGERPRINT,
14: } from '../../mcp_server/lib/validation/spec-doc-structure.js';
15: 
16: const CONTINUITY_STALENESS_THRESHOLD_MS = 10 * 60 * 1000;
17: const FRONTMATTER_RE = /^(?:\uFEFF)?(?:\s*<!--[\s\S]*?-->\s*)*---\s*\r?\n([\s\S]*?)\r?\n---(?:\s*\r?\n|$)/;
18: const COMPLETION_DOCS = [
19:   'spec.md',
20:   'plan.md',
21:   'tasks.md',
22:   'checklist.md',
23:   'decision-record.md',
24:   'implementation-summary.md',
25:   'handover.md',
26: ] as const;
27: 
28: type ResultStatus = 'pass' | 'warn' | 'fail';
29: 
30: export interface ContinuityFreshnessResult {
31:   readonly rule: 'CONTINUITY_FRESHNESS';
32:   readonly status: ResultStatus;
33:   readonly code:
34:     | 'fresh'
35:     | 'fresh_completion'
36:     | 'stale'
37:     | 'content_stale'
38:     | 'dirty_tree'
39:     | 'clock_drift'
40:     | 'no_completion_claim'
41:     | 'missing_fingerprint'
42:     | 'zero_fingerprint'
43:     | 'implementation_summary_missing'
44:     | 'missing_frontmatter'
45:     | 'missing_graph_metadata'
46:     | 'missing_graph_timestamp'
47:     | 'invalid_graph_metadata'
48:     | 'invalid_timestamp'
49:     | 'invalid_frontmatter';
50:   readonly message: string;
51:   readonly details: string[];
52:   readonly continuityTimestamp?: string;
53:   readonly graphTimestamp?: string;
54:   readonly deltaMs?: number;
55: }
56: 
57: interface CliOptions {
58:   folder: string;
59:   json: boolean;
60:   strict: boolean;
61: }
62: 
63: interface ContinuityFields {
64:   timestamp: string | null;
65:   fingerprint: string | null;
66:   completionPct: number | null;
67:   status: string | null;
68:   parseError: string | null;
69: }
70: 
71: interface CompletionCandidate {
72:   basename: string;
73:   filePath: string;
74:   content: string;
75:   fingerprint: string | null;
76:   hasCompletionClaim: boolean;
77:   recomputedFingerprint: string;
78: }
79: 
80: function toErrorMessage(error: unknown): string {
81:   return error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
82: }
83: 
84: function isTruthyEnv(value: string | undefined): boolean {
85:   return ['1', 'true', 'yes', 'on'].includes((value ?? '').toLowerCase().trim());
86: }
87: 
88: function isEnforceMode(): boolean {
89:   return isTruthyEnv(process.env.SPECKIT_COMPLETION_FRESHNESS_ENFORCE);
90: }
91: 
92: function extractFrontmatter(markdown: string): string | null {
93:   return markdown.match(FRONTMATTER_RE)?.[1] ?? null;
94: }
95: 
96: function readContinuityFields(markdown: string): ContinuityFields {
97:   const frontmatterBlock = extractFrontmatter(markdown);
98:   if (!frontmatterBlock) {
99:     return {
100:       timestamp: null,
101:       fingerprint: null,
102:       completionPct: null,
103:       status: null,
104:       parseError: null,
105:     };
106:   }
107: 
108:   try {
109:     const parsed = yaml.load(frontmatterBlock) as Record<string, unknown> | null;
110:     const memory = parsed?._memory as Record<string, unknown> | undefined;
111:     const continuity = memory?.continuity as Record<string, unknown> | undefined;
112:     const sessionDedup = continuity?.session_dedup as Record<string, unknown> | undefined;
113:     const completionPct = continuity?.completion_pct;
114:     const rawStatus = parsed?.status;
115:     const metadataStatus = extractMetadataTableStatus(markdown);
116: 
117:     return {
118:       timestamp: typeof continuity?.last_updated_at === 'string' ? continuity.last_updated_at : null,
119:       fingerprint: typeof sessionDedup?.fingerprint === 'string'
120:         ? sessionDedup.fingerprint
121:         : typeof memory?.fingerprint === 'string'
122:           ? memory.fingerprint
123:           : null,
124:       completionPct: typeof completionPct === 'number'
125:         ? completionPct
126:         : typeof completionPct === 'string'
127:           ? Number.parseFloat(completionPct)
128:           : null,
129:       status: typeof rawStatus === 'string' ? rawStatus : metadataStatus,
130:       parseError: null,
131:     };
132:   } catch (error: unknown) {
133:     return {
134:       timestamp: null,
135:       fingerprint: null,
136:       completionPct: null,
137:       status: null,
138:       parseError: toErrorMessage(error),
139:     };
140:   }
141: }
142: 
143: function extractMetadataTableStatus(markdown: string): string | null {
144:   const match = markdown.match(/^\|\s*\*\*Status\*\*\s*\|\s*([^|]+?)\s*\|\s*$/im);
145:   return match?.[1]?.trim() || null;
146: }
147: 
148: function readContinuityTimestamp(markdown: string): {
149:   timestamp: string | null;
150:   parseError: string | null;
151: } {
152:   const fields = readContinuityFields(markdown);
153:   return { timestamp: fields.timestamp, parseError: fields.parseError };
154: }
155: 
156: function parseTimestamp(value: string): number | null {
157:   const parsed = Date.parse(value);
158:   return Number.isNaN(parsed) ? null : parsed;
159: }
160: 
161: function readGraphMetadataTimestamp(graphMetadataPath: string): {
162:   timestamp?: string;
163:   error?: string;
164: } {
165:   try {
166:     const parsed = JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync(graphMetadataPath, 'utf-8')) as {
167:       derived?: { last_save_at?: unknown };
168:     };
169:     return typeof parsed?.derived?.last_save_at === 'string'
170:       ? { timestamp: parsed.derived.last_save_at }
171:       : {};
172:   } catch (error: unknown) {
173:     return { error: toErrorMessage(error) };
174:   }
175: }
176: 
177: function buildPass(
178:   code: ContinuityFreshnessResult['code'],
179:   message: string,
180:   details: string[] = [],
181:   extra: Partial<ContinuityFreshnessResult> = {},
182: ): ContinuityFreshnessResult {
183:   return {
184:     rule: 'CONTINUITY_FRESHNESS',
185:     status: 'pass',
186:     code,
187:     message,
188:     details,
189:     ...extra,
190:   };
191: }
192: 
193: function buildWarn(
194:   code: ContinuityFreshnessResult['code'],
195:   message: string,
196:   details: string[] = [],
197:   extra: Partial<ContinuityFreshnessResult> = {},
198: ): ContinuityFreshnessResult {
199:   return {
200:     rule: 'CONTINUITY_FRESHNESS',
201:     status: 'warn',
202:     code,
203:     message,
204:     details,
205:     ...extra,
206:   };
207: }
208: 
209: function buildFail(
210:   code: ContinuityFreshnessResult['code'],
211:   message: string,
212:   details: string[] = [],
213: ): ContinuityFreshnessResult {
214:   return {
215:     rule: 'CONTINUITY_FRESHNESS',
216:     status: 'fail',
217:     code,
218:     message,
219:     details,
220:   };
221: }
222: 
223: function isCompletionStatus(status: string | null): boolean {
224:   return /\b(?:complete|completed|done|shipped|implemented)\b/i.test(status ?? '');
225: }
226: 
227: function hasChecklistEvidenceClaim(basename: string, content: string): boolean {
228:   return basename === 'checklist.md'
229:     && /^\s*-\s+\[[xX]\].*(?:\[EVIDENCE:|\|\s*Evidence:|[✓✔☑✅]|\((?:verified|tested|confirmed)\)|\[DEFERRED:)/im.test(content);
230: }
231: 
232: function hasCompletionClaim(basename: string, content: string, fields: ContinuityFields): boolean {
233:   return fields.completionPct === 100
234:     || isCompletionStatus(fields.status)
235:     || hasChecklistEvidenceClaim(basename, content);
236: }
237: 
238: function collectCompletionCandidates(specFolderPath: string): CompletionCandidate[] {
239:   const candidates: CompletionCandidate[] = [];
240:   for (const basename of COMPLETION_DOCS) {
241:     const filePath = path.join(specFolderPath, basename);
242:     if (!fs.existsSync(filePath)) {
243:       continue;
244:     }
245:     const content = fs.readFileSync(filePath, 'utf-8');
246:     const fields = readContinuityFields(content);
247:     candidates.push({
248:       basename,
249:       filePath,
250:       content,
251:       fingerprint: fields.fingerprint,
252:       hasCompletionClaim: hasCompletionClaim(basename, content, fields),
253:       recomputedFingerprint: buildContinuityFingerprint(content),
254:     });
255:   }
256:   return candidates;
257: }
258: 
259: function findGitRoot(folderPath: string): string | null {
260:   try {
261:     return execFileSync('git', ['-C', folderPath, 'rev-parse', '--show-toplevel'], {
262:       encoding: 'utf-8',
263:       stdio: ['ignore', 'pipe', 'ignore'],
264:     }).trim();
265:   } catch {
266:     return null;
267:   }
268: }
269: 
270: function listPacketDirtyPaths(specFolderPath: string): string[] {
271:   const gitRoot = findGitRoot(specFolderPath);
272:   if (!gitRoot) {
273:     return [];
274:   }
275: 
276:   const relativeFolder = path.relative(gitRoot, specFolderPath).replace(/\\/g, '/');
277:   try {
278:     const output = execFileSync('git', ['-C', gitRoot, 'status', '--porcelain', '--', relativeFolder], {
279:       encoding: 'utf-8',
280:       stdio: ['ignore', 'pipe', 'ignore'],
281:     });
282:     return output
283:       .split('\n')
284:       .map((line) => line.trimEnd())
285:       .filter(Boolean)
286:       .map((line) => line.slice(3).trim())
287:       .filter(Boolean);
288:   } catch {
289:     return [];
290:   }
291: }
292: 
293: function evaluateCompletionFreshness(specFolderPath: string): ContinuityFreshnessResult | null {
294:   const completionClaims = collectCompletionCandidates(specFolderPath)
295:     .filter((candidate) => candidate.hasCompletionClaim);
296: 
297:   if (completionClaims.length === 0) {
298:     return buildPass(
299:       'no_completion_claim',
300:       'Continuity freshness skipped: no completion claim found',
301:     );
302:   }
303: 
304:   const candidatesWithFingerprint = completionClaims
305:     .filter((candidate) => candidate.fingerprint && /^sha256:[a-f0-9]{64}$/.test(candidate.fingerprint));
306: 
307:   if (candidatesWithFingerprint.length === 0) {
308:     return buildPass(
309:       'missing_fingerprint',
310:       'Continuity freshness skipped: completion claim has no stored session_dedup fingerprint',
311:     );
312:   }
313: 
314:   const usableCandidates = candidatesWithFingerprint
315:     .filter((candidate) => candidate.fingerprint !== ZERO_CONTINUITY_FINGERPRINT);
316: 
317:   if (usableCandidates.length === 0) {
318:     return buildPass(
319:       'zero_fingerprint',
320:       'Continuity freshness skipped: completion claim has only the zero fingerprint placeholder',
321:     );
322:   }
323: 
324:   const staleCandidates = usableCandidates
325:     .filter((candidate) => candidate.fingerprint !== candidate.recomputedFingerprint);
326:   const dirtyPaths = listPacketDirtyPaths(specFolderPath);
327: 
328:   if (staleCandidates.length > 0 || dirtyPaths.length > 0) {
329:     const details = [
330:       ...staleCandidates.map((candidate) => `${candidate.basename}: stored=${candidate.fingerprint} recomputed=${candidate.recomputedFingerprint}`),
331:       ...dirtyPaths.slice(0, 10).map((dirtyPath) => `dirty=${dirtyPath}`),
332:       'How to Fix: rerun verification, refresh session_dedup.fingerprint, and ensure packet paths are clean.',
333:       'fix: rerun the completion workflow after updating the packet continuity fingerprint.',
334:     ];
335:     const code = staleCandidates.length > 0 ? 'content_stale' : 'dirty_tree';
336:     const message = staleCandidates.length > 0
337:       ? 'Completion freshness is stale: stored continuity fingerprint does not match current content'
338:       : 'Completion freshness is stale: packet paths have uncommitted changes';
339: 
340:     return isEnforceMode()
341:       ? buildFail(code, message, details)
342:       : buildWarn(code, message, details);
343:   }
344: 
345:   return buildPass(
346:     'fresh_completion',
347:     'Completion freshness passed: completion claim fingerprint matches current content and packet paths are clean',
348:     usableCandidates.map((candidate) => `${candidate.basename}: recomputed=${candidate.recomputedFingerprint}`),
349:   );
350: }

<type>file</type>
<content>
1: #!/usr/bin/env node
2: /**
3:  * Ephemeral-pointer audit — enforces sk-code §4 "No Ephemeral-Artifact Pointers".
4:  *
5:  * Scans source files and flags CODE COMMENTS that embed pointers to ephemeral
6:  * artifacts (spec folders/numbers, task/checklist/requirement ids, review-finding
7:  * ids, ADR ids, ticket ids). The durable WHY of a comment is fine; only the
8:  * perishable traceability id is forbidden, because it rots once the spec is
9:  * archived or the review closes.
10:  *
11:  * What it does NOT flag (by design):
12:  *   - String/code content (only comment regions are inspected).
13:  *   - Durable/structural numbers: HTTP status codes, embedding dims, token tiers,
14:  *     rolling-window sizes, schema-version tags ("V16:").
15:  *   - JSDoc @example / parser-format illustrations that show input SHAPE
16:  *     (e.g. "019-system-hardening", "specs/001-demo", a quoted "NNN-name").
17:  *   - Runtime path constants the code actually reads (DEFAULT_ROOT-style lines).
18:  *
19:  * Standalone, dependency-free (Node ESM, stdlib only). Exit nonzero on any hit
20:  * so CI / pre-commit / sk-code verification can gate on it.
21:  *
22:  * Authoritative rule source:
23:  *   .opencode/skills/sk-code/references/universal/code_style_guide.md §4
24:  *
25:  * Usage:
26:  *   node ephemeral-pointer-audit.mjs <path> [<path> ...] [--json]
27:  *   node ephemeral-pointer-audit.mjs .opencode/skills .opencode/bin
28:  *   node ephemeral-pointer-audit.mjs <path> --json
29:  *
30:  * Exit codes: 0 = clean, 1 = violations found, 2 = bad invocation.
31:  */
32: 
33: import fs from "node:fs";
34: import path from "node:path";
35: 
36: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
37: // File selection
38: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
39: 
40: // Extensions whose comment syntax we understand.
41: const SCANNED_EXTENSIONS = new Set([".ts", ".tsx", ".js", ".jsx", ".mjs", ".cjs", ".py"]);
42: 
43: // Directories never worth scanning (vendored / build output / VCS).
44: const SKIP_DIRS = new Set([
45:   "node_modules",
46:   ".git",
47:   "dist",
48:   "build",
49:   "coverage",
50:   ".venv",
51:   "__pycache__",
52:   ".pytest_cache",
53: ]);
54: 
55: // Extensionless files (e.g. .opencode/bin/spec-kit-mcp) are scanned when their
56: // shebang marks them as Node or Python — those use //, /* */, or # comments.
57: const SHEBANG_NODE = /^#!.*\b(node|deno|bun)\b/;
58: const SHEBANG_PY = /^#!.*\bpython[0-9.]*\b/;
59: 
60: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
61: // Forbidden ephemeral-id patterns (matched ONLY inside comment text)
62: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
63: //
64: // Each entry documents WHAT it catches so the allow-list reasoning stays legible.
65: // `name` is reported with the finding; `re` runs against a single comment's text.
66: //
67: // PRECISION OVER RECALL: each pattern is deliberately narrowed to the SHAPE of a
68: // real ephemeral tracking id and explicitly avoids durable look-alikes the live
69: // tree is full of — HTTP-code pairs (401/403), source line ranges (280-316),
70: // token-budget ranges (250-400), ratios (100/100), and externally-versioned
71: // standards (SHA-256, UTF-16, CWE-502, RFC-7231). Those carve-outs live both
72: // here (in the regex itself) and in the ALLOW table below.
73: const FORBIDDEN = [
74:   {
75:     name: "spec-folder-ref",
76:     // "Spec 031", "Phase 005", "Packet 117", "Iteration 12" — an explicit
77:     // tracking-artifact WORD immediately followed by a number. The leading word
78:     // is what makes this unambiguous (a bare number is never flagged here).
79:     re: /\b(?:spec|phase|packet|iteration|sprint)\s+0*\d{1,4}\b/i,
80:   },
81:   {
82:     name: "spec-pair-ref",
83:     // A spec / sub-phase pair: "031/005", "029/001", "005-001", "026/007/013".
84:     // CRITICAL precision constraint: at least ONE side must be a 3-digit
85:     // ZERO-PADDED number (leading 0), which is the spec-folder numbering
86:     // convention. This is what separates a real "029/003" from HTTP "401/403",
87:     // a line range "280-316", or a ratio "100/100" — none of which are
88:     // zero-padded. Optional third segment catches "026/007/013".
89:     re: /\b(?:0\d{2}[\/-]\d{3}|\d{3}[\/-]0\d{2})(?:[\/-]\d{3})?\b/,
90:   },
91:   {
92:     name: "spec-slug",
93:     // "031-embedding-stack-hardening" — 3-digit prefix + a kebab word slug.
94:     // Format-illustration shapes ("019-system-hardening" in an @example,
95:     // "specs/001-demo") are suppressed by the ALLOW table, not here.
96:     re: /\b\d{3}-[a-z][a-z0-9]*(?:-[a-z0-9]+)+\b/,
97:   },
98:   {
99:     name: "task-id",
100:     // Task ids: "T043", "T-123", "T73". Requires the token to be a STANDALONE
101:     // T+digits word (>=2 digits) and NOT immediately preceded by a letter, so
102:     // it never fires inside identifiers. Two-digit floor avoids loop vars (T1).
103:     re: /\bT-?\d{2,}\b/,
104:   },
105:   {
106:     name: "checklist-id",
107:     // "CHK-160", "CHK069" — checklist item ids.
108:     re: /\bCHK-?\d{2,}\b/i,
109:   },
110:   {
111:     name: "requirement-id",
112:     // "REQ-005", "REQ007" — requirement ids.
113:     re: /\bREQ-?\d{2,}\b/i,
114:   },
115:   {
116:     name: "adr-id",
117:     // "ADR-004", "ADR014" — architecture-decision-record ids.
118:     re: /\bADR-?\d{1,}\b/i,
119:   },
120:   {
121:     name: "review-finding-id",
122:     // Deep-review / agent finding tags: "DR-008", "WS-1", "OR-R-01", "F-001-005",
123:     // "P0-3", "P1-09", "P2-14", "DEC-12". Uppercase finding-prefix + dash +
124:     // digits, with optional extra dash-segments ("F-001-005", "A6-P2-2" tail).
125:     // The prefix set is an allow-list of KNOWN review schemes, NOT a generic
126:     // [A-Z]+ catch-all — that is what stops it eating SHA-256 / UTF-16 / CWE-22.
127:     re: /\b(?:DR|WS|OR(?:-R)?|F|P[012]|DEC|FND)-\d{1,}(?:-\d{1,})*\b/,
128:   },
129:   {
130:     name: "github-issue-ref",
131:     // "#456", "(#456)", "Fix #16", "Safeguard #10" — issue/PR references.
132:     // Standalone #-number token only (preceded by start / whitespace / bracket).
133:     re: /(?:^|[\s(\[])#\d{2,}\b/,
134:   },
135: ];
136: 
137: // NOTE intentionally NOT a forbidden rule: a generic "ticket-id" /[A-Z]{3,}-\d/
138: // pattern. It is irresistibly drawn to durable standards the style guide §4
139: // EXPLICITLY allows — SHA-256, UTF-8/16, CWE-502, RFC numbers — and to the
140: // project's own non-ephemeral constants. A real external tracker key (JIRA-123,
141: // CU-8abc) is better caught by an opt-in project-specific prefix list than by a
142: // broad shape that produces a flood of false positives. See notes/wiring.
143: 
144: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
145: // Allowed exclusions — when ANY of these matches a comment, the listed
146: // forbidden rule(s) are suppressed for that comment. These encode the
147: // "durable / structural" carve-outs from the style guide §4.
148: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
149: const ALLOW = {
150:   // Format-illustration / example / JSDoc-shape context: suppress the spec-shaped
151:   // rules. These show input SHAPE, not a live traceability pointer.
152:   //   * @example specs/001-demo/spec.md
153:   //   format: "019-system-hardening" or "NNN-name"
154:   //   // e.g. specs/001-demo   |   // Parses folder paths like "...140-hybrid-rag/006-sprint-5"
155:   exampleContext:
156:     /@example\b|@param\b|@returns?\b|\be\.?g\.?\b|\bi\.?e\.?\b|\bformat\s*[:=]|\bNNN\b|\bpattern\s*[:=]|\bspecs?\/|\bsuch as\b|\bfolder paths? like\b|\blike\s+"|->/i,
157: 
158:   // Schema-version tags ("V16:", "V3:") are durable — never a spec ref.
159:   schemaTag: /\bV\d{1,}\s*:/,
160: 
161:   // Rolling-window descriptors ("200-decision window") are durable config.
162:   rollingWindow: /\b\d{1,4}-decision\b/i,
163: 
164:   // Runtime path constant lines (DEFAULT_ROOT = ".opencode/specs/..."). The path
165:   // is structural — the resolver reads it. Suppress spec-shaped rules on such lines.
166:   runtimePath: /\bDEFAULT_ROOT\b|\.opencode\/specs\b/,
167: 
168:   // Externally-versioned standards explicitly allowed by §4 ("a stable external
169:   // standard ... does not get archived with a sprint"). These collide in shape
170:   // with task/finding ids, so suppress those on any comment naming a standard.
171:   // SHA-256, UTF-8/16, CWE-79/400/502, RFC-7231, ISO-8601, base64, UTF8 BOM, etc.
172:   externalStandard: /\b(?:SHA|MD)-?\d{2,}\b|\bUTF-?\d{1,2}\b|\bUTF8\b|\bCWE-\d{1,}\b|\bRFC-?\d{2,}\b|\bISO-?\d{3,}\b|\bECMA-?\d{2,}\b/i,
173: 
174:   // Source-location references the running code does not chase as a tracking
175:   // artifact: "lines 280-316", "at lines 776-791", "(protocol.js:280-316)".
176:   // A bare numeric range next to "line(s)" or after a "file.ext:" is structural.
177:   // This suppresses spec-pair-ref ONLY (the rule whose shape a range can mimic).
178:   sourceLineRange: /\blines?\b|\.(?:ts|tsx|js|mjs|cjs|jsx|py):\d/i,
179: 
180:   // HTTP status pairs / token-budget ranges / score ratios. "401/403", "250-400",
181:   // "100/100", "404, retry on 500". Numbers here are durable values, not spec ids.
182:   // Recognized when the comment also names HTTP/status/token/budget/error context
183:   // OR when the pair is plainly non-zero-padded (already excluded by the rule's
184:   // own zero-pad constraint, but this catches narration like "map 401/403").
185:   numericValueContext:
186:     /\b(?:HTTP|status|code|token|budget|tier|ms|ratio|score|retry|error|backoff|dims?|dimensions?)\b/i,
187: 
188:   // Internal enumerated safeguards ("Safeguard #10") are durable design labels —
189:   // learned-feedback.ts documents a numbered "10 Safeguards" list — not GitHub issues.
190:   safeguardEnum: /\bsafeguards?\s+#?\d/i,
191: };
192: 
193: // Map each allow-context to the forbidden rule names it is permitted to suppress.
194: // Allow-contexts are deliberately narrow so a real id elsewhere on the line still
195: // fires (e.g. an @example line that ALSO drops a "DR-008" still reports DR-008,
196: // because exampleContext does not suppress review-finding-id).
197: const ALLOW_SUPPRESSES = {
198:   exampleContext: new Set(["spec-slug", "spec-pair-ref", "spec-folder-ref", "github-issue-ref"]),
199:   schemaTag: new Set([]), // schema tags don't look like any forbidden id; informational
200:   rollingWindow: new Set(["spec-pair-ref"]),
201:   runtimePath: new Set(["spec-slug", "spec-pair-ref", "spec-folder-ref"]),
202:   externalStandard: new Set(["task-id", "checklist-id", "requirement-id", "adr-id", "review-finding-id"]),
203:   sourceLineRange: new Set(["spec-pair-ref"]),
204:   numericValueContext: new Set(["spec-pair-ref"]),

#!/usr/bin/env node
/**
 * Ephemeral-pointer audit — enforces sk-code §4 "No Ephemeral-Artifact Pointers".
 *
 * Scans source files and flags CODE COMMENTS that embed pointers to ephemeral
 * artifacts (spec folders/numbers, task/checklist/requirement ids, review-finding
 * ids, ADR ids, ticket ids). The durable WHY of a comment is fine; only the
 * perishable traceability id is forbidden, because it rots once the spec is
 * archived or the review closes.
 *
 * What it does NOT flag (by design):
 *   - String/code content (only comment regions are inspected).
 *   - Durable/structural numbers: HTTP status codes, embedding dims, token tiers,
 *     rolling-window sizes, schema-version tags ("V16:").
 *   - JSDoc @example / parser-format illustrations that show input SHAPE
 *     (e.g. "019-system-hardening", "specs/001-demo", a quoted "NNN-name").
 *   - Runtime path constants the code actually reads (DEFAULT_ROOT-style lines).
 *
 * Standalone, dependency-free (Node ESM, stdlib only). Exit nonzero on any hit
 * so CI / pre-commit / sk-code verification can gate on it.

1: #!/usr/bin/env bash
2: # ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
3: # COMPONENT: Shell Common
4: # ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
5: # Shared shell utilities for spec-kit scripts.
6: # Source this file: source "$(dirname "$0")/../lib/shell-common.sh"
7: #
8: # Functions:
9: # _json_escape()    - Escape strings for safe JSON embedding
10: # Find_repo_root()  - Walk parent directories to find repository root
11: #
12: # Compatibility: Bash 3.2+ (macOS default)
13: # ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
14: 
15: # Conditional strict mode — skipped when sourced to avoid breaking caller's error handling.
16: if [[ "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}" == "$0" ]]; then
17:     set -euo pipefail
18: fi
19: 
20: # Guard against double-sourcing
21: [[ -n "${_SHELL_COMMON_LOADED:-}" ]] && return 0
22: _SHELL_COMMON_LOADED=1
23: 
24: # ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
25: # JSON string escape — handles special characters for safe JSON embedding
26: # Usage: _json_escape "string with special chars"
27: # Canonical version (consolidated from create.sh and validate.sh)
28: # ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
29: _json_escape() {
30:     local str="$1"
31:     # Order matters: backslash first, then other escapes
32:     str="${str//\\/\\\\}"   # Backslash
33:     str="${str//\"/\\\"}"   # Double quote
34:     str="${str//$'\n'/\\n}" # Newline
35:     str="${str//$'\r'/\\r}" # Carriage return
36:     str="${str//$'\t'/\\t}" # Tab
37:     printf '%s' "$str"
38: }
39: 
40: # ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
41: # Phase parent detection — determines whether a folder is a phase
42: # parent (contains at least one populated NNN-* child folder).
43: # Contract: ≥1 direct child matching ^[0-9]{3}-[a-z0-9-]+$ AND
44: # ≥1 such child has spec.md OR description.json.
45: # Usage: is_phase_parent "/path/to/spec-folder"
46: # Returns: 0 if phase parent, 1 if not
47: # ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
48: is_phase_parent() {
49:     local parent_folder="$1"
50:     local has_nnn_child=false
51:     local has_populated_child=false
52: 
53:     for entry in "$parent_folder"/*/; do
54:         [[ -d "$entry" ]] || continue
55:         local dirname
56:         dirname=$(basename "$entry")
57:         if [[ "$dirname" =~ ^[0-9]{3}-[a-z0-9][a-z0-9-]*$ ]]; then
58:             has_nnn_child=true
59:             if [[ -f "$entry/spec.md" ]] || [[ -f "$entry/description.json" ]]; then
60:                 has_populated_child=true
61:                 break
62:             fi
63:         fi
64:     done
65: 
66:     [[ "$has_nnn_child" == "true" ]] && [[ "$has_populated_child" == "true" ]]
67: }
68: 
69: # ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
70: # Repository root detection — walks parent directories looking
71: # For .git or .specify markers.
72: # Usage: find_repo_root "/some/starting/directory"
73: # Returns: Prints repo root path to stdout, returns 0 on success, 1 if not found
74: #
75: # Note: For git-first detection, see also scripts/common.sh get_repo_root()
76: # Which uses `git rev-parse --show-toplevel` as primary strategy.
77: # ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
78: find_repo_root() {
79:     local dir="${1:-}"
80: 
81:     [[ -n "$dir" ]] || return 1
82: 
83:     while [[ "$dir" != "/" ]]; do
84:         if [[ -d "$dir/.git" ]] || [[ -d "$dir/.specify" ]]; then
85:             printf '%s\n' "$dir"
86:             return 0
87:         fi
88:         dir="$(dirname "$dir")"
89:     done
90:     return 1
91: }

95: # 1 - General error

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# COMPONENT: Shell Common
# ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Shared shell utilities for spec-kit scripts.
# Source this file: source "$(dirname "$0")/../lib/shell-common.sh"
#
# Functions:
# _json_escape()    - Escape strings for safe JSON embedding
# Find_repo_root()  - Walk parent directories to find repository root
#
# Compatibility: Bash 3.2+ (macOS default)
# ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

# Conditional strict mode — skipped when sourced to avoid breaking caller's error handling.
if [[ "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}" == "$0" ]]; then
    set -euo pipefail
fi

# Guard against double-sourcing
[[ -n "${_SHELL_COMMON_LOADED:-}" ]] && return 0
_SHELL_COMMON_LOADED=1

# ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# JSON string escape — handles special characters for safe JSON embedding
# Usage: _json_escape "string with special chars"
# Canonical version (consolidated from create.sh and validate.sh)
# ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
_json_escape() {
    local str="$1"
    # Order matters: backslash first, then other escapes
    str="${str//\\/\\\\}"   # Backslash
    str="${str//\"/\\\"}"   # Double quote
    str="${str//$'\n'/\\n}" # Newline
    str="${str//$'\r'/\\r}" # Carriage return
    str="${str//$'\t'/\\t}" # Tab
    printf '%s' "$str"
}

# ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Phase parent detection — determines whether a folder is a phase
# parent (contains at least one populated NNN-* child folder).
# Contract: ≥1 direct child matching ^[0-9]{3}-[a-z0-9-]+$ AND
# ≥1 such child has spec.md OR description.json.
# Usage: is_phase_parent "/path/to/spec-folder"
# Returns: 0 if phase parent, 1 if not
# ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
is_phase_parent() {
    local parent_folder="$1"
    local has_nnn_child=false
    local has_populated_child=false

    for entry in "$parent_folder"/*/; do
        [[ -d "$entry" ]] || continue
        local dirname
        dirname=$(basename "$entry")
        if [[ "$dirname" =~ ^[0-9]{3}-[a-z0-9][a-z0-9-]*$ ]]; then
            has_nnn_child=true
            if [[ -f "$entry/spec.md" ]] || [[ -f "$entry/description.json" ]]; then
                has_populated_child=true
                break
            fi
        fi
    done

    [[ "$has_nnn_child" == "true" ]] && [[ "$has_populated_child" == "true" ]]
}

# ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Repository root detection — walks parent directories looking
# For .git or .specify markers.
# Usage: find_repo_root "/some/starting/directory"
# Returns: Prints repo root path to stdout, returns 0 on success, 1 if not found
#
# Note: For git-first detection, see also scripts/common.sh get_repo_root()
# Which uses `git rev-parse --show-toplevel` as primary strategy.
# ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
find_repo_root() {
    local dir="${1:-}"

    [[ -n "$dir" ]] || return 1

    while [[ "$dir" != "/" ]]; do
        if [[ -d "$dir/.git" ]] || [[ -d "$dir/.specify" ]]; then
            printf '%s\n' "$dir"
            return 0
        fi
        dir="$(dirname "$dir")"
    done
    return 1
}

# Exit codes:
# 0 - Success
# 1 - General error

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Assert every structured doc pointer cited in AGENTS.md resolves on disk.
# A dead pointer in the most-read surface silently misleads readers — and was
# observed to lead a research lineage to a false conclusion — so this turns that
# rot into a fail-loud check rather than a sentence that quietly goes stale.
set -o pipefail

ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")/../../../../.." && pwd)"
AGENTS="$ROOT/AGENTS.md"
SSK="$ROOT/.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit"
BT='`' # literal backtick, held in a variable so it never opens a command substitution

if [ ! -f "$AGENTS" ]; then
  echo "FAIL: AGENTS.md not found at $AGENTS"
  exit 2
fi

tmp="$(mktemp)"
trap 'rm -f "$tmp" "$tmp.dead"' EXIT

display.text:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Assert every structured doc pointer cited in AGENTS.md resolves on disk.
# A dead pointer in the most-read surface silently misleads readers — and was
# observed to lead a research lineage to a false conclusion — so this turns that
# rot into a fail-loud check rather than a sentence that quietly goes stale.
set -o pipefail

ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")/../../../../.." && pwd)"
AGENTS="$ROOT/AGENTS.md"
SSK="$ROOT/.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit"
BT='`' # literal backtick, held in a variable so it never opens a command substitution

if [ ! -f "$AGENTS" ]; then
  echo "FAIL: AGENTS.md not found at $AGENTS"
  exit 2
fi

tmp="$(mktemp)"
trap 'rm -f "$tmp" "$tmp.dead"' EXIT

# Only structured references/constitutional pointers — the char class excludes
# glob/placeholder forms (e.g. NNN-name patterns) so the check never false-fails.
grep -oE "${BT}(\.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/)?(references|constitutional)/[A-Za-z0-9_/.-]+\.md${BT}" "$AGENTS" \
  | tr -d "$BT" | sort -u > "$tmp"

: > "$tmp.dead"
while IFS= read -r p; do
  [ -z "$p" ] && continue
  case "$p" in
    .opencode/*) cand="$ROOT/$p" ;;
    *)           cand="$SSK/$p" ;;
  esac
  [ -f "$cand" ] || echo "DEAD: $p" >> "$tmp.dead"
done < "$tmp"

if [ -s "$tmp.dead" ]; then
  cat "$tmp.dead"
  echo "FAIL: AGENTS.md cites doc pointer(s) that do not resolve"
  exit 1
fi

echo "PASS: all AGENTS.md references/constitutional doc pointers resolve"
exit 0
